topic: AI resumes all sound the same, so recruiters search for proof
thesis: Recruiter-style teardown of an AI-polished resume that misses HubSpot, CAC, LinkedIn Ads, and lifecycle marketing.

sourceNotes.title: YouTube Analytics engagement and retention
sourceNotes.note: Monitor retention and engagement after posting, not just views.

sourceNotes.title: TikTok Creative Center
sourceNotes.note: Use trend discovery to validate current hooks and sounds before publishing.

script.title: The AI Resume Roast: Why Yours Might Be Doomed

chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.content: Open with a joke about sending out 100 applications and hearing crickets—cue the ‘sad trombone’ sound.

chapters.title: The AI Resume Trap
chapters.content: Dive into how AI resumes look polished but lack substance. Show a bland bullet point vs. a detailed one. Instant roast: ‘Does this resume even know what a HubSpot is?’

chapters.title: Rejecting the Vanilla Resume
chapters.content: Point out that vague phrases like 'proficient' do nothing. Cue meme of a recruiter yawning. Joke: ‘If I see one more “team player,” I might need to take a nap!’

chapters.title: The Precision Game
chapters.content: Explain how resumes should be customized to reflect key skills in the job description. Visual of a matchmaking app with resumes and job descriptions—'Your perfect match awaits!'

chapters.title: The Signal Score: Your Secret Weapon
chapters.content: Introduce the Signal score as a free tool to assess resume strength. Visual cue: include a URL overlay for immediate access.

chapters.title: Conclusion and Call to Action
chapters.content: Wrap with a calm reminder to check the Signal score. Joke: ‘It’s free, unlike that therapist you’ve been booking after all those rejections!’

visuals: Boring bullets flash on the screen contrasted with lively, engaging ones.
Signal mascot appearing as a quirky guide popping up with commentary.
Graphs showing engagement or resume stats moving dramatically.

youtube.title: Your AI resume sounds professional. That might be the problem. | Resume Teardown
youtube.seoTitle: Your AI resume sounds professional. That might be the problem. | Resume Teardown | Signal by ATSHacker
youtube.description: A funny recruiter-style teardown of why a qualified resume can be hard to find, how to translate a job description into resume proof, and where Signal fits without fake claims.
youtube.cta: Paste the job description and check your free Signal score before you apply.

scriptSections.label: Cold open
scriptSections.script: Your AI resume sounds professional. That might be the problem. Today we are lovingly roasting a resume that looks professional but has the job-description equivalent of airplane mode turned on.
scriptSections.visual: Cold open with comic red stamp, quick zoom, Signal mascot reaction, and resume/JD split screen.

scriptSections.label: The real problem
scriptSections.script: Most resumes are not failing because the person is unqualified. They fail because the resume says 'helped with stuff' while the job description is asking for a specific toolkit.
scriptSections.visual: Resume on left, job description on right, mismatched terms highlighted with funny labels.

scriptSections.label: Translate the job
scriptSections.script: Before rewriting anything, pull out the role title, tools, responsibilities, metrics, and proof signals the job actually asks for.

scriptSections.label: Teardown
scriptSections.script: Now compare the resume line by line. Vague bullets like 'helped with campaigns' do not give recruiters the same signal as specific tools, scope, and outcomes.

scriptSections.label: Fix one bullet
scriptSections.script: The fix is not to invent experience. The fix is to say the real work clearly: what tool, what scope, what audience, what result.

scriptSections.label: Product bridge
scriptSections.script: Signal automates this comparison. Paste the job description, upload the resume, and see the free score first. If the gap is real, then the paid resume and cover letter bundle makes sense.
scriptSections.visual: Free Signal score page, trust badges, and bundle CTA.

shorts.title: Your AI resume has LinkedIn breath
shorts.hook: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport lounges.
shorts.script: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport lounges. Very polished. Very beige. But the job asks for HubSpot, CAC, and lifecycle marketing, and your resume says results-driven team player. Signal finds the missing role language before you apply.
storyboard: Open with giant text: 'LinkedIn breath detected.'
Show an over-polished AI resume with a beige 'professional yap' stamp.
Cut to job description terms highlighted in yellow.
Signal mascot side-eyes the missing keyword chips.
Reveal free Signal score and CTA.

props.subline: Signal compares resume language to the target job.
props.voiceover_text: Your AI resume sounds like it networks at airport lounges. Very polished. Very beige. But the job asks for HubSpot, CAC, and lifecycle marketing, and your resume says results-driven team player. Signal finds the missing role language before you apply.

shorts.title: Stop writing helped with campaigns
shorts.hook: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache.
shorts.script: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache. 'Helped with campaigns' tells me almost nothing. If you used HubSpot, ran paid social, or moved pipeline, say that clearly. No fake experience. Just stop hiding the good part.
storyboard: Circle weak bullet and stamp 'suspiciously vague'.

props.voiceover_text: This bullet is wearing a fake mustache. 'Helped with campaigns' tells me almost nothing. If you used HubSpot, ran paid social, or moved pipeline, say that clearly. No fake experience. Just stop hiding the good part.

shorts.title: The ATS is not a wizard
shorts.hook: The ATS did not read your mind. Rude, honestly.
shorts.script: The ATS did not read your mind. Rude, honestly. But it usually stores and searches what you actually wrote. If the job says lifecycle marketing and your resume says helped the team, your best experience is hiding in a hoodie.

monetization.cta: Free Signal score -> $9.99 resume or $14.99 bundle.
monetization.upsell: Make the $14.99 bundle the default value offer after score completion.
